Combined T+V Interaction
Ba=3.346 kips ; Bv=1.276 kips	TMS 402-22 Eq 8-8	(kT/N/Ba)5/3+(kV/N/Bv)5/3 = 3.530	≤ 1.00

Engineering Notes:
Design per TMS 402-22 ASD §8.1.4.3. Applies to headed anchor bolts or bent bar anchors (standard hook) fully embedded in grouted masonry.
lbe (edge distance): Per TMS §6.3.7, measured from the edge of the masonry to the center of the bolt — auto-computed as b/2 for a bolt centered in the wall thickness. Override with the manual field if the bolt is positioned differently.
Apt: πlb² per Eq 6-5, less the projected area lying outside the masonry (beyond each wall face) and less overlap with adjacent anchors at spacing S (no portion of masonry counted more than once). Apv: πlbe²/2 per Eq 6-6 (half-cone toward the free edge), with the same overlap adjustment. (TMS §6.3.2, §6.3.3)
Tension (Ba): Lesser of masonry breakout (1.25Apt√f′m) and steel tensile (0.5AbFu). Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi).
Shear (Bv): Minimum of four failure modes: masonry breakout (Bv1), pryout/crushing (Bv2 = 580(Abf′m)0.25), masonry shear cone (Bv3), and steel shear (Bv4 = 0.25AbFu).
Combined Check (Eq 8-8): (k·S·ba/Ba)5/3 + (k·S·bv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0. The ⅓ allowable stress increase, when checked, is applied as k = 3/4 on the demands — consistently in the individual checks and the interaction.
⅓ allowable stress increase (IBC 1605.2) applied: k = 3/4 on demand for all checks including the combined interaction.
Minimum embedment (TMS §6.3.6): Lb,min = greater of 4ϕ and 2″. For 3/4″ bolts: Lb,min = 3″.
Discrete-group applicability: This mode assumes a rigid attachment (plate or HSS base) delivering load through the group centroid, with no group moment or torsion. Eccentric anchor groups are out of scope and require separate analysis.
Equal-share statics: Per-anchor tension = T/N, per-anchor shear = V/N (N = rows × columns = 4). This is an equal-share simplification, not a rigorous elastic group distribution.
Masonry is assumed fully grouted throughout the entire projected breakout region for all 4 anchors in the group.
Shear V is applied in a single, consistently-defined direction (toward the near/loaded free face) for every anchor in the group.
Wall ends, openings, and orthogonal return corners are assumed remote — no anchor in this group is closer than 2lb to any such feature. Verify this against the actual wall plan; this calculator does not check it automatically.
Conservatism: Overlap deductions between anchor breakout cones are computed pairwise; where 3+ cones mutually overlap, pairwise subtraction over-deducts that shared region, so the computed Apt,i/Apv,i are a conservative LOWER BOUND on the true tributary area (never unconservative).

Engineering Notes:
Design per TMS 402-22 ASD §8.1.4.3. Applies to headed anchor bolts or bent bar anchors (standard hook) fully embedded in grouted masonry.
lbe (edge distance): Per TMS §6.3.7, measured from the edge of the masonry to the center of the bolt — auto-computed as b/2 for a bolt centered in the wall thickness. Override with the manual field if the bolt is positioned differently.
Apt: πlb² per Eq 6-5, less the projected area lying outside the masonry (beyond each wall face) and less overlap with adjacent anchors at spacing S (no portion of masonry counted more than once). Apv: πlbe²/2 per Eq 6-6 (half-cone toward the free edge), with the same overlap adjustment. (TMS §6.3.2, §6.3.3)
Tension (Ba): Lesser of masonry breakout (1.25Apt√f′m) and steel tensile (0.5AbFu). Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi).
Shear (Bv): Minimum of four failure modes: masonry breakout (Bv1), pryout/crushing (Bv2 = 580(Abf′m)0.25), masonry shear cone (Bv3), and steel shear (Bv4 = 0.25AbFu).
Combined Check (Eq 8-8): (k·S·ba/Ba)5/3 + (k·S·bv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0. The ⅓ allowable stress increase, when checked, is applied as k = 3/4 on the demands — consistently in the individual checks and the interaction.

Minimum embedment (TMS §6.3.6): Lb,min = greater of 4ϕ and 2″. For 3/4″ bolts: Lb,min = 3″.
Discrete-group applicability: This mode assumes a rigid attachment (plate or HSS base) delivering load through the group centroid, with no group moment or torsion. Eccentric anchor groups are out of scope and require separate analysis.
Equal-share statics: Per-anchor tension = T/N, per-anchor shear = V/N (N = rows × columns = 4). This is an equal-share simplification, not a rigorous elastic group distribution.
Masonry is assumed fully grouted throughout the entire projected breakout region for all 4 anchors in the group.
Shear V is applied in a single, consistently-defined direction (toward the near/loaded free face) for every anchor in the group.
Wall ends, openings, and orthogonal return corners are assumed remote — no anchor in this group is closer than 2lb to any such feature. Verify this against the actual wall plan; this calculator does not check it automatically.
Conservatism: Overlap deductions between anchor breakout cones are computed pairwise; where 3+ cones mutually overlap, pairwise subtraction over-deducts that shared region, so the computed Apt,i/Apv,i are a conservative LOWER BOUND on the true tributary area (never unconservative).

Engineering Notes:
Design per TMS 402-22 ASD §8.1.4.3. Applies to headed anchor bolts or bent bar anchors (standard hook) fully embedded in grouted masonry.
lbe (edge distance): Per TMS §6.3.7, measured from the edge of the masonry to the center of the bolt — auto-computed as b/2 for a bolt centered in the wall thickness. Override with the manual field if the bolt is positioned differently.
Apt: πlb² per Eq 6-5, less the projected area lying outside the masonry (beyond each wall face) and less overlap with adjacent anchors at spacing S (no portion of masonry counted more than once). Apv: πlbe²/2 per Eq 6-6 (half-cone toward the free edge), with the same overlap adjustment. (TMS §6.3.2, §6.3.3)
Tension (Ba): Lesser of masonry breakout (1.25Apt√f′m) and steel tensile (0.5AbFu). Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi).
Shear (Bv): Minimum of four failure modes: masonry breakout (Bv1), pryout/crushing (Bv2 = 580(Abf′m)0.25), masonry shear cone (Bv3), and steel shear (Bv4 = 0.25AbFu).
Combined Check (Eq 8-8): (k·S·ba/Ba)5/3 + (k·S·bv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0. The ⅓ allowable stress increase, when checked, is applied as k = 3/4 on the demands — consistently in the individual checks and the interaction.
⅓ allowable stress increase (IBC 1605.2) applied: k = 3/4 on demand for all checks including the combined interaction.
Minimum embedment (TMS §6.3.6): Lb,min = greater of 4ϕ and 2″. For 3/4″ bolts: Lb,min = 3″.
Discrete-group applicability: This mode assumes a rigid attachment (plate or HSS base) delivering load through the group centroid, with no group moment or torsion. Eccentric anchor groups are out of scope and require separate analysis.
Equal-share statics: Per-anchor tension = T/N, per-anchor shear = V/N (N = rows × columns = 4). This is an equal-share simplification, not a rigorous elastic group distribution.
Masonry is assumed fully grouted throughout the entire projected breakout region for all 4 anchors in the group.
Shear V is applied in a single, consistently-defined direction (toward the near/loaded free face) for every anchor in the group.
Wall ends, openings, and orthogonal return corners are assumed remote — no anchor in this group is closer than 2lb to any such feature. Verify this against the actual wall plan; this calculator does not check it automatically.
Conservatism: Overlap deductions between anchor breakout cones are computed pairwise; where 3+ cones mutually overlap, pairwise subtraction over-deducts that shared region, so the computed Apt,i/Apv,i are a conservative LOWER BOUND on the true tributary area (never unconservative).

Engineering Notes:
This calculator implements Strength Design (SD) per TMS 402-22 Chapter 9 for headed anchor bolts. For Allowable Stress Design (ASD), use TMS 402-22 Chapter 8 (Eq. 8-1 through 8-7, with Bvc = 580 × ⁴√(f’m × Ab)).
Masonry crushing (Eq. 9-5): Bvnc = 1750 × (f’m × Ab)1/4. This is the 4th root of (f’m × Ab).
Projected areas are enumerated for every bolt per TMS 402-22 §6.3.2/§6.3.3. Every other bolt closer than 2lb deducts a full circSeg term at its actual center distance from Apt; every other bolt closer than 2lbe deducts a half circSeg term from Apv. Pairwise deduction is a conservative lower bound because deducted regions may overlap.
Edge distance lbe for shear should reflect the effective grouted zone limit when only partial grouting is present (not the full edge distance to the parapet top).
ASCE 7-22 Section 14.4.5.1 requires increasing factored design forces by 2.0 when a non-ductile (masonry-controlled) failure mode governs in SDC D or higher. This calculator does not automatically apply this amplification — verify with the project seismic design category.
This calculator does not design the steel ledger angle, welds, or joist seat. See MDG REK-08 SD for full shear connection design discussion.
